Facilitator Training Program  ·  School & Community Settings  ·  Evidence-Based

Therapeutic Drumming Interventions

Facilitator Certification Program

Tom Harris, Founder & Master Facilitator 48 Years of Practice Douglasville, GA
Not a drum circle. A structured, trauma-informed therapeutic intervention.

Rhythm is not a supplement to healing. It is the mechanism.

TDI uses rhythm, percussion, and West African drumming traditions to build self-regulation from the inside out. Structured, evidence-based, and designed for schools, correctional facilities, and community healing spaces — with measurable behavioral outcomes verified by school data.

About the Training Program

11 Months. 12 Training Days. One Certification.

The TDI Facilitator Certification Program equips practitioners to bring therapeutic drumming into school and community settings with confidence and fidelity. Training takes place across 12 intensive days spread over 11 months — combining live instruction, hands-on drumming practice, supervised implementation, and guided reflection.

11
Months
12
Training Days
280
Page Manual
10
Core Modules

Program Structure

Phase 1

Foundations — Classroom instruction + intensive drumming practice. Introduction to TDI frameworks, West African traditions, and trauma-informed facilitation.

Phase 2

Supervised Implementation — Facilitated sessions with real populations under Tom’s guidance. Skill refinement through direct practice and structured observation.

Phase 3

Certification — Independent facilitation, outcome documentation, and completion of the 280-page TDI Training Manual. Ongoing support available post-certification.

Who Should Attend

  • School counselors, social workers, and behavioral interventionists
  • Special education and alternative education staff
  • PBIS / MTSS coordinators and behavioral health teams
  • Youth workers, case managers, and community practitioners
  • Therapists and counselors working with trauma-affected populations
  • Music therapists and arts-based practitioners

What’s Expected of You

Full commitment to all 12 training days. Active participation is required — this is a hands-on practitioner training.

Willingness to drum. No prior musical experience needed — just show up ready to play.

Implementation between sessions — practice with real students or community members as part of the supervised phase.

Facilitator Certification — Training Details
Page 2 of 2

10-Point Training Curriculum

01
Therapeutic Drumming Foundations
History, theory, and TDI framework overview
02
Rhythm as Regulatory Mechanism
Neuroscience of rhythm and the nervous system
03
Trauma-Informed Facilitation
Bottom-up healing, safety, trauma-sensitive practice
04
West African Drumming Traditions
Cultural context, roots, rhythmic vocabulary
05
Session Design & Sequencing
Structure, flow, adaptive facilitation
06
Group Dynamics & Observation
Reading the room, de-escalation tools
07
MTSS / PBIS Integration
Aligning TDI with school behavioral frameworks
08
Student Assessment & Monitoring
Observational tools and outcome tracking
09
Cultural Humility in Healing
Equity, posture, community-centered practice
10
Documentation & Outcome Reporting
Data for school, district, and grant reporting

Program Investment

Training (up to 10 participants)Per day of instruction$800 / day
TDI Training Manual280-page evidence-based curriculum$75
Michael Meade Audio SeriesRecommended supplemental resource$57
Drum Build WorkshopOptional — participants build their own djembe$425

Custom pricing available for larger cohorts or multi-day intensives. Contact Tom directly to discuss scheduling.

What to Expect

  • Hands-on drumming from day one — no experience required
  • Small cohort (up to 10) for individualized learning
  • Direct mentorship from Tom across all 12 training days
  • Supervised real-world implementation with feedback
  • Completion of the 280-page TDI Training Manual
  • Certification upon completion with ongoing support

Nash County Schools  ·  75 students  ·  one school year

About Tom Harris

Tom Harris has spent 48 years bringing therapeutic drumming into schools, correctional facilities, and community healing spaces. His approach integrates West African drumming traditions with trauma-informed, bottom-up healing frameworks — producing measurable, school-verified outcomes.

Tom’s 280-page training manual is in use by facilitators across the country. His work has been publicly endorsed by Michael Mead, renowned mythopoetic educator and author.

Ready to become a TDI facilitator?
